title: Cacio e Pepe
description: The classic Roman pasta dish reduced to its bare essentials — cheese, pepper, and pasta water.
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package directions until just shy of al dente, reserving 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
Meanwhile, toast the black pepper in a large skillet over medium heat until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add 1/3 cup of pasta water and bring to a simmer.
Add the drained pasta to the skillet and toss to coat. Remove from heat.
Add both cheeses gradually, tossing constantly and adding pasta water a splash at a time to create a creamy, emulsified sauce. The key is to keep tossing — don't let it clump.
Serve immediately with extra pepper and cheese.
notes: Use freshly cracked pepper — pre-ground won't give you the same punch. The pasta water is everything here; starchy water is what makes the sauce creamy without cream.

title: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Charred chicken in a rich, warmly-spiced tomato cream sauce. Better than takeout and not much harder.
ingredients: 1.5 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into 2-inch pieces
Fresh cilantro and basmati rice to serve
instructions: Combine chicken with yogurt, tikka masala spice, half the garlic, half the ginger, salt and pepper. Marinate at least 1 hour or overnight.
Broil the marinated chicken on a foil-lined baking sheet at high heat for 10-12 minutes until charred in spots. Set aside.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook onion until soft and golden, about 10 minutes. Add remaining garlic and ginger, cook 2 minutes more.
Add crushed tomatoes and simmer 10 minutes until thickened. Stir in heavy cream and garam masala.
Add the broiled chicken and simmer 5 minutes. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve over basmati rice, topped with fresh cilantro.
notes: The broiling step is key — don't skip the char. Marinating overnight makes a big difference. Works well with cauliflower instead of chicken for a vegetarian version.
